title: Lastrea scabra
intro: Lastrea scabra, also known as the scabrous lastrea or scabrous lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to reduce symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea scabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including pain, inflammation, and respiratory problems.
uses: Lastrea scabra is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating fever and rheumatism. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Lastrea scabra.
